records.ety: [OE. marou, mary, maruh, AS. mearg, mearh; akin to OS. marg, D. merg, G. Mark, OHG. marg, marag, Icel. mergr, Sw. merg, Dan. marv, Skr. majjan; cf. Skr. majj to sink, L. mergere. √274 Cf. Merge.]

defs: The tissue which fills the cavities of most bones; the medulla. In the larger cavities it is commonly very fatty, but in the smaller cavities it is much less fatty, and red or reddish in color.

quotes: It takes from our achievements . . . The pith and marrow of our attribute.

defs: One of a pair; a match; a companion; an intimate associate.
quotes: Chopping and changing I can not commend, With thief or his marrow, for fear of ill end.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Marrowed (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Marrowing.]

defs: To fill with, or as with, marrow or fat; to glut.
